assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-doc, non-testing tasks (Tasks 1-5) all contain: Repository ('trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description, at least one of Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements. Task 1 has Files to Create and Files to Modify plus Implementation Notes. Task 2 has Files to Create and Files to Modify plus Implementation Notes. Task 3 has Files to Modify and Implementation Notes. Task 4 has Files to Modify and Implementation Notes. Task 5 has Files to Create and Files to Modify plus Implementation Notes. Documentation task (Task 6) has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements and correctly omits Files to Modify/Create and Implementation Notes. Testing tasks (Tasks 7-8) have Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements and correctly omit Files to Modify/Create and Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 12 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: 8 task files exist: task-1-severity-model-and-service.md, task-2-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-3-cache-invalidation.md, task-4-threshold-query-param.md, task-5-integration-tests.md, task-6-documentation-update.md, task-7-smoke-tests.md, task-8-performance-benchmarks.md. 8 is within the range [3, 12].
All 8 task files contain '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' as their first section. Verified in task-1 through task-8.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 2. Task 5 depends on Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4. Task 6 depends on Tasks 1, 2, 4, 5. Task 7 depends on Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Task 8 depends on Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. This is a valid DAG with topological ordering 1 -> 2 -> 3, 4 -> 5 -> 6, 7, 8. No circular dependencies exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths reference existing manifest paths: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, tests/Cargo.toml — all present in repo-backend.md. Task 4 modifies files created by earlier tasks (advisory_summary.rs) in directories that exist in the manifest. All Files to Create paths are new files in existing manifest directories: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/, tests/api/.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map (impact-map.md) lists 7 specific changes including: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct to represent aggregated severity counts (critical, high, medium, low, total)', 'Add severity aggregation method to SbomService that queries the sbom_advisory join table, deduplicates by advisory ID, and groups counts by severity level', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler with 5-minute tower-http cache configuration and 404 response for nonexistent SBOM IDs'. All items name specific structs, methods, endpoints, tables, and behaviors.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' — a single repository name with no other repository names mentioned in the Repository section.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 task files contain all four required sections: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified across task-1 through task-8.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths that exist in the manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (manifest line 41), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (manifest line 46), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (manifest line 49), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (manifest line 83), tests/Cargo.toml (manifest line 114). Files to Create are new files in existing manifest directories (modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/, tests/api/). Task 4 modifies files created by Tasks 1 and 2 in the same plan, not invented paths.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-documentation, non-testing task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance. Documentation tasks and testing tasks are exempt from this requirement as they intentionally omit Implementation Notes
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Implementation Notes reference: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs (SbomSummary), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs (SbomDetails), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, entity/sbom_advisory.rs, entity/advisory.rs, and specific patterns like 'Result<T, AppError>' and 'COUNT(DISTINCT advisory_id)'. Task 2 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, common/src/error.rs, and pattern '.route("/api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary", get(get_advisory_summary))'. Task 3 references: modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/sbom/mod.rs with specific patterns. Task 4 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, 'Query<ThresholdParams>'. Task 5 references: tests/api/sbom.rs, tests/api/advisory.rs, entity/sbom_advisory.rs, 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)' pattern. Tasks 6, 7, 8 omit Implementation Notes (exempt).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 task files contain '## Target Branch\nmain'. Verified in task-1-severity-model-and-service.md (line 5), task-2-advisory-summary-endpoint.md (line 5), task-3-cache-invalidation.md (line 5), task-4-threshold-query-param.md (line 5), task-5-integration-tests.md (line 5), task-6-documentation-update.md (line 5), task-7-smoke-tests.md (line 5), task-8-performance-benchmarks.md (line 5).

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 digest files (task-1-digest.md through task-8-digest.md) contain '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<computed-after-jira-creation>' — the value '<computed-after-jira-creation>' is a placeholder string, NOT 64 lowercase hex characters. The assertion requires exactly 64 lowercase hex characters after the 'sha256-md:' prefix, not a placeholder. No actual SHA-256 hash is present in any digest file.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All conventions included in Implementation Notes have prescribed-format rationales. Examples: Task 1: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs` matching the convention's Rust service file scope.' and 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs` matching the convention's Rust model file scope.' Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust endpoint registration scope.' Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust source file scope.' No 'Not applicable' annotations found in any task. Inapplicable conventions like §Migration Patterns are absent from all tasks (none touch migration files).

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map (impact-map.md, lines 64-72) includes 'additional_fields: { "priority": {"name": "Major"} }' and states 'Priority: "Major" — inherited from Feature (not "Undefined", so propagated)'. Summary comment (summary-comment.md, line 47) states 'Priority: "Major" -- inherited from Feature TC-9001 and propagated to all created Epics and Tasks'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map (impact-map.md, lines 64-72) includes 'additional_fields: { "fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}] }' and states 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.5.0"] — inherited from Feature. No `fixVersion scope` setting found in Jira Field Defaults, defaulting to "both" (propagated to tasks)'. Summary comment (summary-comment.md, line 47) confirms: 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.5.0"] -- inherited from Feature TC-9001 and propagated to all created Epics and Tasks (no `fixVersion scope` setting in Jira Field Defaults; defaulted to "both")'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an 'Inherited Field Values' section stating: 'Priority: "Major" -- inherited from Feature TC-9001 and propagated to all created Epics and Tasks' and 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.5.0"] -- inherited from Feature TC-9001 and propagated to all created Epics and Tasks'. The impact map also contains a 'Jira Field Inheritance' section with the same information.

assertion_results.text: A documentation task is generated because the feature description's Documentation Considerations section indicates doc impact (Updates — add endpoint to REST API reference). The documentation task includes Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies sections, and omits Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, and Verification Commands
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (task-6-documentation-update.md) is the documentation task. It includes: Repository ('trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description (covers updating REST API reference for the new endpoint), Acceptance Criteria (4 checkbox items), Test Requirements (3 checkbox items), Dependencies (depends on Tasks 1, 2, 4, 5). It omits all required sections: no '## Files to Modify', no '## Files to Create', no '## API Changes', no '## Implementation Notes', no '## Reuse Candidates', no '## Verification Commands' sections are present.

assertion_results.text: Testing tasks are generated from the testing readiness template — exactly 2 testing tasks are created (one per test category: Smoke Tests and Performance Benchmarks). Each testing task includes Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies sections, and omits Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, and Verification Commands
assertion_results.evidence: Exactly 2 testing tasks exist: Task 7 (task-7-smoke-tests.md) for 'Smoke Tests' and Task 8 (task-8-performance-benchmarks.md) for 'Performance Benchmarks', matching the 2 categories in docs/testing-readiness.md. Task 7 includes: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria (3 items matching the smoke test template), Test Requirements (5 items), Dependencies (Tasks 1-5). Task 7 omits: Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Verification Commands. Task 8 includes: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria (3 items matching the performance benchmarks template), Test Requirements (5 items), Dependencies (Tasks 1-5). Task 8 omits: Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Verification Commands.

assertion_results.text: Each testing task depends on all implementation tasks (not on documentation or other testing tasks)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 7 (Smoke Tests) depends on: Task 1, Task 2, Task 3, Task 4, Task 5 — all 5 implementation tasks. Task 8 (Performance Benchmarks) depends on: Task 1, Task 2, Task 3, Task 4, Task 5 — all 5 implementation tasks. Neither Task 7 nor Task 8 depends on Task 6 (documentation) or on each other (no cross-testing-task dependencies).
